scope_description: 5-generation Jewish diaspora tree where the same DNA relative appears on AncestryDNA, MyHeritage, GEDmatch and FTDNA under different names, while one same-surname match is a different person. Designed to validate match identity resolution, cluster mapping, platform conflict handling and endogamy-aware evidence scoring.

input_dna_matches.expected_link: same person cluster as dna_902/dna_903; descends from Sara Levitin, sister of Abram Levitin
same name but different person; should not merge with dna_901/902/903
same Levitin/Kaplan cluster but distinct person from Adrienne Kaplan
Galician Kaplan/Rosenberg cluster, not Levitin/Kaplan cluster

input_archive_snippets.transcription_excerpt: Birth of Abram Levitin, son of Hersh Levitin and Freida Friedman.
Birth of Sara Levitin, daughter of Hersh Levitin and Freida Friedman.
input_archive_snippets.expected_use: confirms Sara Levitin as sister of Abram; key link to Kaplan descendants.

input_archive_snippets.transcription_excerpt: Marriage of Meyer Kaplan of Kobryn and Sara Levitin of Brest-Litovsk, daughter of Hersh Levitin.
input_archive_snippets.expected_use: connects Kaplan line to Levitin branch.

input_archive_snippets.transcription_excerpt: Rachel Kaplan, born Warsaw, last residence London, contact: daughter Adrienne Kaplan.
A. Kaplan and Geoff Michael merged as same person due to shared matches and overlapping surnames.
input_archive_snippets.expected_use: should be flagged as erroneous same-cluster-not-same-person merge.

input_archive_snippets.transcription_excerpt: A. Kaplan FTDNA kit B904904 claims Levitin line, but tree shows Galician Rosenberg/Kaplan and no Brest/Levitin ancestors.
input_archive_snippets.expected_use: conflicting same-name match; identity resolver should keep separate from Adrienne cluster.

input_user_assertions.assertion: A. Kaplan on Ancestry and Adrienne K. on MyHeritage are probably the same person.
input_user_assertions.evidence: same email hash, same tree surnames, same cM, same shared matches

input_user_assertions.assertion: A. Kaplan on FTDNA is also the same person because name matches.
Geoff M. may be the same person as Adrienne because he shares the same triangulated segment.

embedded_errors.reason: same display name but different email hash, cM level, shared matches, surnames and cluster
triangulation supports same family cluster, not same individual
small Galician Kaplan/Rosenberg match should not be absorbed into Levitin/Kaplan cluster
public tree merged A. Kaplan and Geoff Michael due to shared matches
Kaplan is common and cannot resolve identity alone

expected_reasoning_chain: Step 1: Parse GEDCOM and identify Abram Levitin and Sara Levitin as sibling candidates.
Step 2: Use src_901 and src_902 to confirm Abram and Sara share parents.
Step 3: Use src_903 to connect Sara Levitin to Meyer Kaplan; this creates expected Kaplan/Levitin descendant branch.
Step 4: Build cross-platform DNA identity graph from match names, email hashes, platform IDs, kit IDs, cM values, shared matches, tree surnames and triangulation.
Step 5: Cluster dna_901, dna_902 and dna_903 as same individual identity: Adrienne Kaplan, because email hash, cM, shared matches and tree surnames align.
Step 6: Keep dna_905 Geoff M. as distinct individual in same family cluster, because email/person profile differs but triangulated segment and shared matches support relatedness.
Step 7: Reject merging dna_904 with Adrienne cluster: same display initials but different email hash, low cM, different shared matches and Galician Rosenberg/Kaplan tree.
Step 8: Apply Ashkenazi endogamy correction to dna_904/dna_906; keep as separate weak Galician Kaplan/Rosenberg cluster.
Step 9: Flag src_905 public tree merge as erroneous same-cluster-not-same-person.
Step 10: Output resolved match identities and relationship hypotheses for each platform match.

identity_cluster_Adrienne_Kaplan.reason: same email hash + consistent cM + shared matches + surnames + GEDmatch kit

identity_dna_905_same_as_Adrienne.reason: same cluster but different person; distinct email/profile and lower cM

identity_dna_904_same_as_Adrienne.reason: same display name only; evidence conflicts

relationship_proband_to_Adrienne_cluster.relationship_summary: descendant of Sara Levitin, sister of proband's Levitin ancestor

relationship_proband_to_Geoff_Michael.relationship_summary: same Levitin/Kaplan collateral cluster; exact branch still requires archive/tree confirmation

cluster_dna_904_dna_906.relationship_summary: separate Galician Kaplan/Rosenberg cluster; weak endogamous signal

true_lineage: Sara Levitin, sister of Abram, married Meyer Kaplan
false_claims: All A. Kaplan matches across platforms are the same person.
Geoff M. is same person as Adrienne because he triangulates on same segment.
Small dna_906 match belongs to the Levitin/Kaplan cluster.

evaluation_assertions.description: Engine must cluster dna_901/dna_902/dna_903 as same person.
Engine must keep dna_905 as same family cluster but different person.
Engine must reject dna_904 as same person as Adrienne cluster.
Engine must confirm relationship to Adrienne cluster through Sara Levitin.
Engine must flag public tree same-cluster-person merge error.
